overview: This U/B/G Blue Tempo deck leverages early disruption and efficient threats to control the pace of Vintage games. By holding up mana for Flusterstorm, it denies opponents their key plays while establishing board presence with Psychic Frog. Collector Ouphe provides a resilient stabilizer that generates card advantage, allowing the deck to outlast aggressive strategies and race control opponents.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage and disruption
Closing games with efficient, hard-to-remove threats
Disrupting opponent's game plan until they run out of resources

keep_criteria: Hands containing early disruption like Flusterstorm
Hands with Psychic Frog or Collector Ouphe alongside lands
Hands with a clear path to disrupt the opponent's first turn
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing fetch/shock or duals for U/B/G), at least one 1-mana interaction (Flusterstorm, Psychic Frog, or Collector Ouphe), and a curve of 1-2-3 spells.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction or early threats
Hands with too many lands or too few
Hands without a clear plan to establish tempo
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, zero interaction, or multiple 3+ mana spells without early support or land acceleration.

early_game: Play lands and hold up two blue mana for Flusterstorm
Cast Psychic Frog to generate card advantage and apply immediate pressure
Disrupt opponent's early threats or combo pieces
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Psychic Frog or Collector Ouphe if mana allows.
Turn 2: Curve into a 2-drop (e.g., Thoughtseize, Go Blank, or a 2-drop threat).
Sequence lands to ensure you can produce U for counters, B for discard, and G for ramp/threats.
Hold up interaction for turn 1/2 threats or key combo pieces, and use Psychic Frog’s tap ability to dig for answers when your hand is light.
mid_game: Deploy Collector Ouphe to stabilize and gain card advantage
Use removal and counterspells to control the board
Maintain tempo by trading efficiently and keeping the initiative
avoid blowing counters on non-critical spells.
Use Psychic Frog’s draw effect to dig for answers when your hand is empty.
Manage your graveyard carefully against Dredge/Oath, and avoid unnecessary triggers that could fuel opponent strategies or reveal your hand to discard.
Commit threats only when you have interaction to protect them or clear the way.
Hold back if the opponent has a known sweep or heavy removal.
late_game: Leverage card advantage to outvalue opponents
Use resilient threats to close the game
Maintain interaction to prevent opponent's late-game finishers
Late-game threats like Tarmogoyf, Scavenging Ooze, or midrange creatures backed by card draw.
Win by out-resourcing and applying pressure when interaction is exhausted, forcing the opponent to use remaining resources inefficiently.
When the opponent has no interaction left, or when you have card advantage and can trade up.
Go all-in if you can close the game before they can stabilize or find a sweep, especially if you have a proven win condition in hand.

key_cards.name: Psychic Frog
key_cards.notes: Cast early to draw cards and apply immediate board presence, often forcing opponent to answer before developing their game plan.

key_cards.name: Collector Ouphe
key_cards.notes: Use to recover from board wipes or aggressive starts, generating value while providing a resilient threat.

key_cards.name: Flusterstorm
key_cards.notes: Hold up two blue mana to counter the first spell cast each turn, protecting your board and denying opponent's key plays.

sequencing_tips: Always prioritize holding up mana for Flusterstorm before committing to other spells
Cast Psychic Frog before playing lands if possible to maximize card draw timing
Use Collector Ouphe after taking damage or losing cards to maximize its value generation
Collector Ouphe is technically a white card, so ensure your mana base supports W if it’s in the deck, or note that the archetype may actually be U/B/W (Esper) but the prompt specifies U/B/G.
Psychic Frog and Flusterstorm are blue, so U is primary.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without interaction, as tempo decks die to sweepers.
Always prioritize card advantage in the late game, and use Psychic Frog’s draw effect strategically to dig for answers rather than wasting it on non-critical spells.

matchups.advice: Use Flusterstorm aggressively to disrupt combo pieces and buy time for your threats.
Prioritize early disruption and use Psychic Frog to generate value while racing.
Apply consistent pressure with threats and use Flusterstorm to protect your board state.

Use Psychic Frog’s draw to dig through, cast spells in response to their counters, or use uncounterable threats. Sequence around known counters and save interaction for their key spells. Force them to use counters on low-impact spells to open up your hand.

Early interaction to stop mana dorks/land drops. Collector Ouphe shuts down non-artifact ramp. Discard their ramp spells and pressure them before they can cast big threats. Don’t let them stabilize; trade efficiently and punish missed blocks.

Win the interaction war. Out-value them with better sequencing, card draw, and sideboard cards. Prioritize keeping interaction for their key threats and force them to make the first mistake. Track their hand and graveyard to anticipate their plays.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Supreme Verdict, Anger of the Gods), graveyard strategies (Dredge, Oath), and artifact engines (Jewel Shops, Initiative).
Note when they tap out for big spells or when they start drawing multiple cards.
Track their remaining interaction to time your own plays and identify when they are vulnerable to pressure.
